text.title: Per-Directory Coverage Rule
text.summary: The pair is per-source-directory, not per-repo: every meaningful folder gets calibrated context so deep-cwd agents do not rebuild the world.

section.title: When a directory MUST have its own pair
section.p: An agent that opens a session with cwd in `src/auth/` should not need to climb the tree to find context. The pyramid is therefore replicated per directory with non-trivial logic; only empty or near-empty folders inherit from the parent.

rule.statement: A directory MUST carry its own `CLAUDE.md` + `AGENTS.md` when ANY of these hold: it contains 3+ source files, it is a package boundary (own `pyproject.toml`/`package.json`/`go.mod`), it has commands or gotchas distinct from its parent, or it is referenced by another `AGENTS.md` as a navigation target. A directory MAY skip the pair only when it is an `__init__.py`-only folder, a generated/build output dir, a vendored dependency, or a leaf with under three files and zero distinct commands.
rule.rationale: Per-directory coverage means an agent always reads exactly the brief that matches its working directory; without it, agents either auto-load the whole repo's context or guess. The skip rules prevent the convention from becoming busywork on trivial folders that would only repeat the parent.

example.code: nero-core/ ├── CLAUDE.md # @AGENTS.md ├── AGENTS.md # repo-wide brief ├── src/ │ ├── auth/ │ │ ├── CLAUDE.md # @AGENTS.md │ │ └── AGENTS.md # auth-specific commands + gotchas │ └── billing/ │ ├── CLAUDE.md │ └── AGENTS.md └── build/ # generated, no pair (skip rule)
example.note: Each package boundary has its own brief; the build folder is exempt as generated output.

example.code: nero-core/ ├── CLAUDE.md # 600 lines covering every package └── src/ ├── auth/ # no pair, agents must climb └── billing/ # no pair, agents must climb
example.note: Single mega-brief at the root; agents working in `src/billing/` load 600 lines of mostly-auth context.

antipattern.description: Adding `CLAUDE.md` + `AGENTS.md` to every leaf folder regardless of content (including empty `__init__.py`-only dirs).
antipattern.reason: Doubles maintenance cost without information gain; each pair must be touched on every refactor. The skip rules exist to keep the convention adoptable, not to be ignored.

section.reference: OpenAI Codex — reads `AGENTS.md` per directory at session start.
Cursor Rules — also follow per-directory `AGENTS.md` discovery.
